Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • How Iran's Proxies Were Impacted During Operation Roaring Lion: Iranian Funding, Training, and Arming from North to South Israel Defense Forces 09 Apr 2026

  • IRGC: Iranian forces launched no attacks during ceasefire hours Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guard Corps (IRGC) has categorically denied carrying out reported drone and missile strikes on facilities in countries along the southern edge of the Persian Gulf, stressing that Iranian forces carried out no such operations during the ceasefire hours.
  • April 9: Iran-US ceasefire in place but already fraying as Israeli aggression continues Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- A day after Washington announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, accepting Tehran's 10-point proposal as a "workable basis" for talks, day 41 of the US-Israeli war opened under a cloud of uncertainty as Israel immediately shattered the truce with deadly strikes on Lebanon, killing over 250 civilians.

  • Trump Warns Tehran Against Imposing Tolls For Hormuz Shipping RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump rejected comments by Tehran that it would begin charging fees to tankers traveling through the Strait of Hormuz, which is considered a free waterway under international law.
  • Trump Says US Military To Remain Near Iran Until 'Real Agreement' Reached RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has said that American forces will remain in place "in, and around, Iran" until a "real agreement" is reached, as a fragile cease-fire between the United States and Iran hangs in the balance largely due to what Washington has labeled a "misunderstanding" over whether the truce includes Israel's offensive against Tehran-backed Hezbollah assets in Lebanon.
  • Trump says Lebanon not included in 2-week ceasefire deal People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that Lebanon is not included in the U.S.-Iran two-week ceasefire.
  • White House says U.S. working on modified plan ahead of talks with Iran People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said Wednesday that Washington is working on a modified peace plan ahead of closed-door talks beginning soon in Islamabad.

  • Statement by PM Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 09 Apr 2026 -- "Israel supports President Trump's decision to suspend strikes against Iran for two weeks subject to Iran immediately opening the straits and stopping all attacks on the US, Israel and countries in the region."

  • Qalibaf: Iran's only battlefield is defending the nation's rights Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Parliamentary Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has reiterated that the only battlefield for Iran is centered on defending the rights of the Iranian people, emphasizing a dual strategy that unites military and diplomatic efforts as essential tools of resistance.
  • Iran FM warns US against allowing Netanyahu to 'crater its economy' Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned the United States against allowing Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu to derail diplomacy amid the talk of possible ceasefire talks between Tehran and Washington.
  • Iran slams UN for inaction in stopping US-Israeli aggression Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi has sharply criticized the United Nations for its failure to act in halting the US-Israeli aggression and for its inability to hold the aggressors accountable.
  • Leader: Iran's heroic people definitive victors in third imposed war Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- New Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei has called for ongoing public engagement in the fight against US and Israeli aggression, praising the Iranian people's "clear victory" in the ongoing imposed war.
  • Enemy agreed to ceasefire based on Iran conditions after failure of war goals: Army spokesman Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- A spokesperson for Iran's Army says the enemy agreed to the Islamic Republic's 10-point proposal for a ceasefire with the United States since it failed to achieve its declared and covert objectives in the war.
  • Passage Through Strait of Hormuz Possible If US Complies With Its Obligations - Araghchi Sputnik 09 Apr 2026 -- Safe passage of vessels through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible if the United States complies with its obligations, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Thursday.

  • China opposes dissemination of speculative and insinuating disinformation targeting China: defense ministry responds to allegations Chinese entities supported Iran Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- China firmly opposes the dissemination of speculative and insinuating disinformation targeting China by relevant parties, Chinese Defense Ministry spokesperson Zhang Xiaogang said on Thursday, in response to a reporter's inquiry regarding reports that claimed Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC) had provided chip manufacturing equipment to the Iranian military, and a Chinese commercial satellite company had released images of US military bases in the Middle East.
  • Chinese FM spokesperson responds to report that China encouraged Iran to accept ceasefire, promised to act as guarantor Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- In response to a media inquiry regarding a report by The Guardian that Pakistani officials said China had directly encouraged Iran to accept a ceasefire and promised to act as a guarantor of Iran's safety in any talks — including assurances that Iranian officials would not be assassinated if they traveled for future negotiations — Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Thursday that China has consistently advocated for an early ceasefire and an end to the fighting, and for disputes to be resolved through political and diplomatic means so as to ultimately achieve long-term peace and stability in the Middle East and the Gulf region, according to The Paper.
  • China maintains communication with all parties: FM on White House' claims of high-level talks between US and China on Iran issue Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- Since the eruption of the war, China has remained in communication with all relevant parties and has been actively promoting peace and preventing escalation of the war, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said at a regular press briefing on Thursday, in response to media inquiry over the White House's claims that the US and China had high level talks on the Iran issue.

  • Press statement by the Federal Chancellor on the domestic and foreign policy situation - "The first ray of hope" Germany - Federal Government 09 Apr 2026 -- Press statement by the Federal Chancellor on the domestic and foreign policy situation
  • Britain's Starmer, In Call With Trump, Speaks Of Need To Restore Hormuz Shipping RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on April 9 spoke with US ⁠President Donald Trump on the need to formulate ⁠a plan to restore shipping "as quickly as possible" through the crucial Strait of Hormuz, Downing Street said.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Foreign Minister of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026
  • Germany to resume direct talks with Iran to support ceasefire: Merz Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 09 Apr 2026 -- German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Thursday announced that the German government would resume direct talks with Iran to support the newly agreed two-week ceasefire between Washington and Tehran, Xinhua reported.
  • Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the EU on the ceasefire agreed by the United States and Iran EC/CEU 09 Apr 2026
  • Statement from the General Secretariat of the GCC Gulf Cooperation Council 09 Apr 2026
  • Houthi: Ceasefire a 'major victory' for Iran, Ummah in fight against US, Israel Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Leader of Yemen's Ansarallah movement Abdul-Malik al-Houthi says the declaration of a ceasefire in the US-Israeli war of terrorism is, in itself, a significant victory for the Islamic Republic of Iran and the Resistance Axis.
  • Foreign Minister Receives Phone Call from Iranian Counterpart to Discuss Regional Stability SPA 09 Apr 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah received a phone call from Foreign Minister of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi, during which they discussed current developments and measures to de-escalate tensions to help restore security and stability in the region
  • HRH the Crown Prince Meets with British Prime Minister SPA 09 Apr 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, met today in Jeddah with British Prime Minister Keir Starmer
  • Foreign Minister, Pakistani Counterpart Discuss Efforts to Restore Regional Security and Stability SPA 09 Apr 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah received a phone call today from Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan Ishaq Dar
  • Japan-Oman Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 09 Apr 2026

  • Strait Of Hormuz 'Toll' Could Breach Maritime Law, Industry Insiders Say RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- Amid reports Iran and the US are considering charging vessels a "toll" to transit the Strait of Hormuz, maritime insiders say such a move could backfire if other countries decide to charge ships for passing through natural maritime chokepoints.
  • Allison Minor: Cease-Fire Or Strategic Pause? Lebanon, Hormuz Test The Iran Truce RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- Forty-eight hours into a US-Iran cease-fire, the situation on the ground already appears increasingly unstable.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • US senate moves once again to curb Trump's war powers amid failure in Iran war Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- The US Senate plans once again to curb the war powers of American President Donald Trump in an attempt to halt his unprovoked war on Iran, which has already failed in its objectives despite heavy expenditure.

  • Army launches data operations center to give warfighters the decisive edge US Army 09 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Army announced the launch of the Army Data Operations Center, or ADOC, a key initiative designed to transform how the Army manages and utilizes its vast data resources to ensure decision dominance on the modern battlefield.
  • Washington Guard, Thai partners strengthen port emergency response Army News 09 Apr 2026 -- Soldiers from the Washington Army National Guard trained alongside Thai partners during a weeklong subject matter expert exchange at the Port of Laem Chabang and Sri Racha Hospital, March 2-9, strengthening emergency response coordination at one of Southeast Asia's busiest maritime hubs.
  • Nimitz Carrier Strike Group trains with Ecuador Navy SOUTHCOM 09 Apr 2026 -- The Ecuadorian and U.S. Navies conducted a bilateral maritime engagement as part of U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command/U.S. 4th Fleet's Southern Seas 2026 deployment in the Pacific Ocean, April 7-8.
  • US Supercharges Tomahawk Orders Amid Iran War Drain Sputnik 09 Apr 2026 -- 'The US Navy has requested $3 billion to procure 785 Tomahawk cruise missiles for fiscal year 2027, a review of Pentagon budget documents shows.
  • Ecuador and U.S. Navies Conduct Bilateral Maritime Engagement in Pacific Ocean US Navy 09 Apr 2026 -- The Ecuadorian and U.S. Navies conducted a bilateral maritime engagement as part of U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command/U.S. 4th Fleet's Southern Seas 2026 deployment in the Pacific Ocean, April 7-8.
  • FIDAE 2026 kicks-off highlights U.S.-Chile airpower, partnership SOUTHCOM 09 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Air Force and Space Force returned to Santiago, Chile in Feria Internacional del Aire y del Espacio (FIDAE) 2026, hosted by the Chilean Air Force (FACh) April 7, 2026.

  • NATO Chief Warns Of Deepening Russia-Iran Cooperation Fueling Middle East Instability RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte on April 9 said growing cooperation between Russia and Iran is strengthening both countries' military capabilities, with direct consequences for the war in Ukraine and rising instability across the Middle East.
  • Shaheen, Tillis Lead Bipartisan Senators in Reaffirming Bipartisan Support for NATO US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 09 Apr 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, and Thom Tillis (R-NC), co-chair of the Senate NATO Observer Group, Susan Collins (R-ME), Lisa Murkowski (R-AK), Jeff Merkley (D-OR), Chris Coons (D-DE), Angus King (I-ME), Cory Booker (D-NJ), Jacky Rosen (D-NV) and John Curtis (R-UT) issued a statement reaffirming the bipartisan commitment in Congress to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization.
  • Trump Blasts NATO After Meeting With Alliance Chief Rutte RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump again blasted NATO, saying the alliance members weren't there "when we needed them," shortly after he met in Washington with ⁠Secretary-General Mark ⁠Rutte.
  • Trump slams NATO again for being unhelpful for U.S. People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- "NATO wasn't there when we needed them, and they won't be there if we need them again," U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday.

  • Pacific Fleet's detachment leaves for Gulf of Thailand after business call at port of Preah Sihanouk of Kingdom of Cambodia Russia MoD 09 Apr 2026 -- The day before, a detachment of ships of the Pacific Fleet consisting of corvettes Sovershenny, Rezky, and the medium sea tanker Pechenga completed a business call at the port of Preah Sihanouk of the Kingdom of Cambodia

  • Brave1 Defense Tech Valley 2026: Ukraine is shaping a new global standard for defense solutions Ukraine MOD 09 Apr 2026 -- Preparations are underway in Ukraine for Brave1 Defense Tech Valley, the largest defense technology event. In 2026, the event will convene manufacturers, investors, and international partners and serve as a platform for scaling Ukrainian solutions globally.
  • Drone Line: implementing a new warfare doctrine Ukraine MOD 09 Apr 2026 -- Drone Line, a large-scale project initiated by the President of Ukraine, continues to be rolled out across the Defence Forces. The objective of the project is to scale up a new doctrine of technology-driven warfare.
  • A bold assault "warhorse" that can swim: BTR-4 Bucephalus overview Ukraine MOD 09 Apr 2026 -- The development of the BTR-4 Bucephalus (Ukrainian: БТР-4 «Буцефал», romanized: Butsefal) armored personnel carrier (APC) by Ukrainian designers marked an important step away from Soviet-era design standards toward modern Western approaches to crew protection and firepower effectiveness. This armored personnel carrier is in service with the Security and Defence Forces of Ukraine and has demonstrated its effectiveness in combat.

  • Regular Press Briefing of the Ministry of National Defense on April 9, 2026 PRC MND 09 Apr 2026
  • China-provided Type 056 corvette part of co-op program with Cambodia, not directed at third party: Chinese Defense Ministry Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- The Type 056 corvette China provided to Cambodia, which has reportedly arrived at the Ream Naval Base, is part of an existing cooperation program between militaries of the two countries, a spokesperson for Chinese Defense Ministry said on Thursday in response to a related question. A Chinese expert told the Global Times that the Type 056 corvette, a light multi-purpose vessel, is expected to enhance Cambodia's maritime security and rescue capabilities, marking further advancement in China-Cambodia defense cooperation.

  • Stuart fires up for Kakadu Australia DOD 09 Apr 2026 -- For HMAS Stuart, leading the final division of ships into Sydney Harbour for the Exercise Kakadu Fleet Review was just the start of the ship's Kakadu contribution.

  • N. Korea says it tested ballistic missile tipped with cluster-bomb warhead Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 09 Apr 2026 -- North Korea said Thursday it tested several weapons systems earlier this week, including a tactical ballistic missile tipped with a cluster-bomb warhead, an electromagnetic system and carbon fiber sham bombs, Kyodo reported citing the state-run media.
  • DPRK Defence Science Research Institutes Conduct Tests of Important Weapon Systems KCNA 09 Apr 2026 -- The Academy of Defence Science and the Missile Administration of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea conducted the tests of electromagnetic weapon system, carbon fiber sham bombs scattering, verifying combat reliability of mobile short-range anti-aircraft missile system and estimating combat application and power of cluster bomb warhead of tactical ballistic missile on April 6, 7 and 8.

  • Japan's Ambition for Military Expansion Laid Bare: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 09 Apr 2026 -- "Japan's reckless and dangerously accelerated push for remilitarization is spiraling out of control. This is a complete betrayal of its pacifist Constitution and 'exclusively defense-oriented policy', laying bare its ambition for military expansion," said Senior Colonel Zhang Xiaogang, spokesperson for China's Ministry of National Defense (MND), at a press briefing on Thursday.
  • Japan to add 17 airports, ports for defense use Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 09 Apr 2026 -- The Japanese government has decided to add 17 airports and ports to its list of designated facilities to allow the Self-Defense Forces and the Japan Coast Guard to use them from peacetime onward as part of efforts to strengthen the country's defenses, Jiji Press reported.
  • Japan gov't designates 17 airports, ports for possible defense use People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- The Japanese government has added 17 airports and ports to its list of designated facilities to allow possible use by the Self-Defense Forces (SDF) and the Japan Coast Guard (JCG), as part of efforts to strengthen the country's defense capabilities, local media reported Thursday.

  • The two-day national seminar on Multi Domain Operations - Ran Samwad kicks off in Bengaluru India PIB 09 Apr 2026 -- The second edition of Ran Samwad, a tri-service strategic seminar, under the aegis of Headquarters Integrated Defence Staff (IDS) commenced in Bengaluru on April 9, 2026.
  • INS SUNAYNA (IOS SAGAR) DEPARTS MALÉ, MALDIVES India PIB 09 Apr 2026 -- The Indian Ocean Ship SAGAR (INS Sunayna) departed from the port of Malé on 08 Apr 2026, further reinforcing the maritime partnership between India and Maldives. The visit was marked by a series of professional engagements and cultural exchanges between the Indian Navy and the Maldives National Defence Force (MNDF).

  • London Says It Tracked Three Russian Submarines Near Critical Undersea Infrastructure RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- British authorities say they have tracked three Russian submarines operating near the United Kingdom in what officials described as a suspected intelligence-gathering mission targeting critical undersea cables and energy infrastructure, according to reporting by international media.
  • UK exposes covert Russian submarine operation in and around UK waters UK MOD 09 Apr 2026 -- The British military has exposed a covert Russian submarine operation in and around UK waters, forcing the vessels to retreat back to Russia.
  • Defence Secretary No9 speech - 09 April 2026 UK MOD 09 Apr 2026 -- The Defence Secretary held a media briefing at No9 today to give an operational update on Russian activity in the Atlantic.
  • £50 million boost for defence businesses in Plymouth and the South West with new defence growth deal UK MOD 09 Apr 2026 -- New defence investment will help defence businesses grow, by cutting red tape and creating hundreds of well-paid jobs, showing how defence is an engine for growth.

  • Statement from Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik regarding Russian patrols in, and near, Norwegian and British maritime areas Government of Norway 09 Apr 2026 -- Statement from Defence Minister Tore O. Sandvik following the press conference of British Defence Minister John Healey on April 9, 2026.

  • Ministerial Committee on Economic Policy agrees on applying for additional funding for detecting and countering drones Finnish Government 09 Apr 2026 -- In its meeting on 9 April, the Ministerial Committee on Economic Policy decided that Finland would apply for EUR 35 million in additional funding from the European Commission's Border Management and Visa Policy Instrument (BMVI).
  • Finland Procures More 155 mm K9 Self-Propelled Howitzers from the Republic of Korea Finnish MoD 09 Apr 2026 -- The Finnish Ministry of Defence and Korea Trade-Investment Promotion Agency (KOTRA) signed a government-to-government contract on 9 April 2026 regarding the purchase of surplus K9 self-propelled howitzers.

  • PCG activates major command post in Kalayaan Island on Day of Valor Philippine News Agency 09 Apr 2026 -- Several government officials on Thursday visited the Pag-asa Island in Kalayaan, Palawan, to celebrate the Day of Valor and to officially activate the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) District Kalayaan Island Group (KIG).
  • 'Salaknib' troops hold helicopter sling-load, anti-armor drills Philippine News Agency 09 Apr 2026 -- Filipino and American troops participating in this year's "Salaknib" exercises conducted helicopter sling-load operations and anti-armor drills, the Philippine Army (PA) said Thursday.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Bulgarian Prime Minister Gurov US Dept. of State 09 Apr 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Romanian Foreign Minister Toiu US Dept. of State 09 Apr 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Mexican Foreign Secretary Velasco US Dept. of State 09 Apr 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Iraqi Ambassador Khirullah US Dept. of State 09 Apr 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Statement by PM Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 09 Apr 2026 -- "In light of Lebanon's repeated requests to open direct negotiations with Israel, I instructed at the Government meeting yesterday to open direct negotiations with Lebanon as soon as possible."
  • Statement by Prime Minister Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 09 Apr 2026 -- "I wish to inform you: There is no ceasefire in Lebanon. We are continuing to strike Hezbollah with full force, and we will not stop until we restore your security."

  • 4 Palestinians, including journalist, killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza City: sources People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- Four Palestinians, including a journalist, were killed on Wednesday in two separate incidents of Israeli airstrikes on Gaza City, according to Palestinian security and medical sources.

  • Israel And Lebanon To Hold Talks As Strikes Cloud US-Iran Cease-Fire RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- Israeli and Lebanese representatives will meet in Washington next week, a State Department official said hours after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called for talks with Lebanon, where a heavy Israeli bombardment targeting Hezbollah militants has shaken the two-week US-Iran cease-fire deal.

  • April 9: Hezbollah's operations against Zionist regime in response to truce breach Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Hours after Iran and the United States announced a ceasefire ending 40 days of aggression against the Islamic Republic, the Israeli regime violated the truce on Wednesday by launching devastating strikes on civilian areas across Lebanon.
  • Lebanon declares national mourning day following deadly Israeli strikes Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 09 Apr 2026 -- Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam announced Wednesday that Thursday, April 9, will be observed as a national day of mourning for the victims and injured in Israeli attacks that targeted hundreds of civilians, Xinhua reports.
  • Hezbollah returns fire with fire; vows to sustain strikes until US-Israeli aggression ends Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Lebanon's Hezbollah resistance movement has unleashed a rocket barrage towards an illegal settlement lying on the northern side of the occupied territories in response to earlier deadly Israeli strikes.

  • Vance Claims Lebanon Is Not Part of the Ceasefire Sputnik 09 Apr 2026 -- Halting hostilities in Lebanon is not part of the ceasefire agreement reached between the United States and Iran, Vice President JD Vance claimed o Wednesday, further calling the matter a "misunderstanding".

  • Lebanon inseparable part of Iran's 10-point ceasefire plan: Qalibaf Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says Lebanon is an inseparable part of the 10-point ceasefire plan the country has proposed, warning that any violation carries strong responses.
  • Qalibaf: 'Time running out' for US-Israel to honor ceasefire, stop attacks on Lebanon Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- Speaker of Iran's Parliament Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf has warned the United States and the Israeli regime that "time is running out," stressing that any attempt to exclude Lebanon or the Axis of Resistance from the recently brokered ceasefire will meet a firm and decisive response.
  • President Pezeshkian: Iran will never abandon Lebanon in face of Israeli aggression Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says Iran will never abandon its Lebanese brothers and sisters following ruthless Israeli bombing of residential buildings despite a ceasefire.

  • Lebanon: Health system overwhelmed following a 'horrific' day of Israeli strikes UN News 09 Apr 2026 -- The scale and speed of destruction from the wave of airstrikes in Lebanon which began just hours after the US-Iran ceasefire announcement, has left the country's already strained health system struggling to cope, according to the World Health Organization (WHO).
  • UNICEF statement on intensified strikes on Lebanon and the devastating impact on children UNICEF 09 Apr 2026

  • Pakistan Condemns Israeli Aggression Against Lebanon Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026 -- Pakistan condemns, in the strongest terms, the ongoing Israeli aggression against Lebanon, resulting in the loss of innocent lives and the widespread destruction of infrastructure.
  • Lebanon part of Iran-US truce deal: Pakistani ambassador to Washington Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- The Pakistani ambassador to Washington has stressed that Lebanon is included in the ceasefire agreement reached through his country's mediation between Iran and the United States.

  • Russia Condemns Israeli Strikes, Says Cease-Fire Deal Must Include Lebanon RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- Russia vocally criticized Israeli strikes on Lebanon, saying they "sharply increase the risk of a resumption of large-scale armed confrontation in the Middle East" and that the Pakistan-brokered cease-fire deal between the US and Iran must include Lebanon.
  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on the developments in Lebanon Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026
  • PH concerned over escalation of conflict in Lebanon Philippine News Agency 09 Apr 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Thursday expressed "grave concern" over the escalation and worsening humanitarian situation in Lebanon after Israel mounted deadly airstrikes in central Beirut on Wednesday, killing over 200.
  • Statement by Minister Anand on the situation in Lebanon Canada 09 Apr 2026
  • Lebanon - Israeli strikes France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026 -- "France condemns in the strongest terms the massive strikes carried out on 8 April by Israel on Lebanon, including many districts of Beirut."
  • France slams 'unacceptable' Israeli attacks as UK says Lebanon should be included in ceasefire Press TV 09 Apr 2026 -- France has censured the latest Israeli atrocities against civilians across Lebanon as unacceptable, whilst the United Kingdom wants the Arab country included in the ceasefire between the US and Iran.
  • Lebanon's sovereignty, security should not be violated: Chinese FM on Iran closing Strait of Hormuz after Israeli strikes Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Thursday that Lebanon's sovereignty and security should not be violated, and the lives and property of civilians must be protected.
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Eide on the Israeli attacks in Lebanon Government of Norway 09 Apr 2026 -- 'I condemn the massive Israeli attacks in Lebanon, which have caused immense suffering and destruction. A great many of the attacks took place without warning across the country, whilst children and ordinary people were going about their daily lives. In the space of 24 hours, nearly 200 people were killed and 1,000 injured. Civilians, healthcare workers and hospitals have been hit hard.
  • Head of Palestine and Occupied Arab Territories Sector convenes with European Union Special Envoy for Middle East Peace Process League of Arab States 09 Apr 2026 -- His Excellency Ambassador Dr Fayed Mustafa, Assistant Secretary-General and head of the Palestine and Occupied Arab Territories Sector, received Mr Christophe Bigot, European Union Special Envoy for the Middle East Peace Process, along with his accompanying delegation at the headquarters of the General Secretariat of the League of Arab States on 9 April.
  • Statement by the Tripartite Mechanism on Israeli attacks in Occupied Jerusalem League of Arab States 09 Apr 2026 -- the General Secretariat of the League of Arab States, the General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation, and the Commission of the African Union expressed their strong condemnation of the storming of the courtyards of Al-Aqsa Mosque by the extremist "minister" in the Israeli occupation government, Itamar Ben-Gvir, under heavy protection from Israeli occupation forces. They considered this act a provocation to the sentiments of Muslims worldwide, a flagrant assault on the sanctity of Al-Aqsa Mosque, and a grave violation of relevant international resolutions and conventions.
  • No: 69, 9 April 2026, Regarding the Approval by the Netanyahu Government of 34 New Settlements in the West Bank Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026
  • Attacks on Lebanon must end, and a ceasefire must apply to all Republic of Slovenia 09 Apr 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, UN humanitarian flights UNOCHA 09 Apr 2026 -- Massive Lebanon airstrikes isolate communities, restricts aid

  • Counting on external forces to stir up troubles at sea by the Philippines will prove self-defeating: Defense Spokesperson PRC MND 09 Apr 2026 -- China urges the Philippine side to stop counting on external forces to stir up troubles at sea, since such a scheme will prove self-defeating and fruitless, said a Chinese defense spokesperson at a press briefing on Thursday.
  • Counting on External Forces to Stir up Troubles at Sea by the Philippine Side Will Prove Self-defeating Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- Question: The Philippine Navy reportedly claimed that China may be taking advantage of the international attention on the conflict in the Middle East, citing a so-called escalation of harassment activities in the South China Sea as proof. The Philippine National Maritime Council also condemned the Chinese side for so-called dangerous maneuvers against Philippine vessels and personnel in the South China Sea. Do you have any comment on this?

  • Security Council Committee on Libya Discusses Final Report of Panel of Experts UN Security Council 09 Apr 2026 -- On 24 March 2026, the Security Council Committee established pursuant to resolution 1970 (2011) concerning Libya held informal consultations to consider the final report of its Panel of Experts, submitted pursuant to paragraph 23 of resolution 2769 (2025).

  • Kosovo mission head warns 'mistrust' threatens post-election stability UN News 09 Apr 2026 -- Despite successful legislative elections in Kosovo late last year, a "delicate equilibrium" persists as deep divisions remain over the future of the United Nations presence in the region.
  • Trust among Communities Allows Progress, Cooperation to Take Root, Kosovo Mission Chief Tells Security Council as Pristina, Belgrade Diverge on Way Forward UN Security Council 09 Apr 2026 -- While successful legislative elections in Kosovo at the close of 2025 featured broad participation, the Security Council today heard that challenges for minority communities there remain, as members discussed the future of the United Nations' presence in the region.
  • EU Statement - UN Security Concil Briefing on UNMIK (Kosovo) European External Action Service (EEAS) 09 Apr 2026 -- Statement on behalf of the European Union and its Member States by H.E. Ambassador Stavros Lambrinidis, Head of the Delegation of the European Union to the United Nations, at the UN Security Council Briefing on Security Council resolutions 1160 (1998), 1199 (1998), 1203 (1998), 1239 (1999) and 1244 (1999)
  • We continue to call on Kosovo and Serbia to resolve outstanding disagreements through the EU-facilitated Dialogue: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 09 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Jennifer MacNaughtan, UK Minister Counsellor, at the Security Council meeting on Kosovo.
  • 27th Anniversary of the NATO Response to the Humanitarian Crisis in Kosovo: UK statement to the OSCE, March 2026 UK FCDO 09 Apr 2026 -- UK Chargé d'Affaires, Deputy Ambassador James Ford, speaks on the anniversary of NATO's response to the humanitarian crisis in Kosovo and underlines the UK's focus on building modern, forward-looking partnerships with countries across the Western Balkans region.

  • Connecticut Woman Sentenced to Prison for Lying to Obtain U.S. Citizenship After Committing Torture and War Crimes in Bosnia US Dept. of Justice 09 Apr 2026 -- A Connecticut woman who was a naturalized U.S. citizen from Bosnia and Herzegovina was sentenced yesterday to 30 months in prison for naturalization fraud based on lies about her prior criminal conduct to obtain U.S. citizenship.

  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna meeting with the President of Armenia and the Speaker of Parliament: the regional peace process is an important opportunity Estonia MFA 09 Apr 2026 -- Today, 9 April, in Yerevan, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna met with the President of Armenia Vahagn Khachaturyan and the Speaker of Parliament Alen Simonyan to discuss bilateral relations and regional security, including the peace process.

  • Escalating violence creates child trafficking crisis in South Sudan, says UN expert OHCHR 09 Apr 2026 -- A UN expert today expressed grave concern about the current escalation of conflict related trafficking in South Sudan.

  • Funding shortfalls put lifelines at risk for Sudanese refugees in Chad WFP 09 Apr 2026 -- More than a million Sudanese refugees in Chad face immediate and life-threatening cuts to food, water, shelter, protection and health care, two leading UN humanitarian agencies warned today as the conflict in neighbouring Sudan approaches the three year mark.

News Reports

  • An Audacious Cyber Hack By Russian Military Intelligence Sets Off Global Alarms. Again. RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- Hackers connected to Russia's military intelligence agency snuck into thousands of Internet routers used in homes and offices worldwide and secretly monitored e-mail traffic and website log-ons and passwords for months, authorities said.

  • Chairman Crawford Joins Former Speaker Gingrich to Discuss U.S. Counterintelligence Reform US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 09 Apr 2026 -- House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence Chairman Rick Crawford (AR-01) joined former Speaker of the House of Representatives Newt Gingrich on his podcast, "Newt's World," to discuss the need to reevaluate how the U

  • Statement by Deputy Foreign Minister of the Russian Federation Dmitry Lyubinsky at the plenary session of the High-Level Conference of the International Conference on Humanitarian Action, Remedy and Responsibility in unilateral sanctions environment, Geneva, 9 April, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's greetings to Secretary General of the Commonwealth of Independent States Sergey Lebedev Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026
  • Nobel-Winners Memorial Labeled 'Extremist' By Russian Court RFE/RL 09 Apr 2026 -- Russia's Supreme Court has allowed the Nobel Peace Prize-winning rights group Memorial to be labeled as an "extremist" organization, a move that will allow authorities to go after anyone who contributes to the work of the group or shares materials it has published.
  • Strategy of blatant abuse of counter-terrorism and extremism laws to destroy Russian civil society must end: UN experts OHCHR 09 Apr 2026 -- UN Special Rapporteurs today condemned the continued strategy by Russian authorities to silence dissent, human rights advocacy and anti-war expression and suppress and destroy civil society in the guise of fighting threats to national security and public safety. They issued the following statement regarding key court hearings in April 2026:
  • Russia - Fundamental freedoms and rights France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 09 Apr 2026 -- "France strongly denounces the Russian Supreme Court's designation of a supposed 'international public movement Memorial', classed as an 'extremist organization'."

  • Xi stresses deepening political rectification at training session for military officials People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday urged efforts to deepen political rectification in the military to maintain the purity and glory of the people's armed forces.
  • China's anti-graft chief stresses high-quality disciplinary inspection for sound five-year plan launch People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- China's top anti-graft official Li Xi on Wednesday called for high-quality disciplinary inspection work to ensure a sound start to the 15th Five-Year Plan period (2026-2030).
  • China launches new internet satellite group Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- China sent a new satellite group into space from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center in north China's Shanxi Province on Thursday.

  • MAC criticizes KMT's Cheng for echoing CCP narrative during China trip CNA 09 Apr 2026 -- Taiwan's Mainland Affairs Council (MAC) urged Kuomintang (KMT) Chairperson Cheng Li-wun (鄭麗文) not to echo the Chinese side's narrative after remarks she made about Dr. Sun Yat-sen (孫中山) in Nanjing during her ongoing visit to China.
  • KMT chair calls for peace as 'greatest gift' during Shanghai visit CNA 09 Apr 2026 -- The leader of Taiwan's main opposition party Kuomintang (KMT) on Thursday emphasized peace as the foundation for cross-strait prosperity, saying it is the "greatest gift" she hopes to take back to Taiwan during a visit to Shanghai.
  • PLA resolute to crush "Taiwan Independence" secession delusions: spokesperson PRC MND 09 Apr 2026 -- The Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) will resolutely crush the secessionist delusions of "Taiwan independence" and defend the national sovereignty and territorial integrity, a military spokesperson said on Thursday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 09 Apr 2026
  • DPP's servility will not get Taiwan any mercy: mainland spokesperson People's Daily 09 Apr 2026 -- Taiwan compatriots are increasingly aware that the United States advances its interests through pressure and power politics, without regard for fairness or reason, a mainland spokesperson said on Wednesday.

  • Chinese FM says strategic guidance of top leaders greatest political advantage for developing China-DPRK ties Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Thursday that the strategic guidance of top leaders of the two parties and countries is the greatest political advantage and strongest guarantee for the development of relations between China and the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK).
  • Chinese FM says China to work with DPRK to consolidate positive momentum of ties Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said on Thursday that China stands ready to work with the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) to continue consolidating the positive momentum in the development of China-DPRK relations.
  • China, DPRK to discuss issues of mutual concern: spokesperson on FM Wang Yi's visit Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- In response to a media inquiry for more information of the visit by Wang Yi, a member of the Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China Central Committee and Chinese Foreign Minister, to the DPRK at the invitation of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the DPRK, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said at Thursday's press conference that, "Yesterday I introduced the relevant arrangements for Foreign Minister Wang Yi's visit to the DPRK. If there is any further information, we will release it in a timely manner, and you may also follow our updates.

  • 7 Middle Eastern states 'prioritize' crude oil supply to Korea KOREA.net 09 Apr 2026 -- Ambassadors from seven Middle Eastern countries on April 7 reaffirmed the commitment of their governments to "prioritize the supply of crude oil from the Middle East to Korea" in a meeting with the National Assembly's Foreign Affairs and Unification Committee.
  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (April 9, 2026) ROK MFA 09 Apr 2026

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Indian Foreign Secretary Misri US Dept. of State 09 Apr 2026

  • Japan's Ambition for Military Expansion Laid Bare Global Times 09 Apr 2026 -- Question: According to reports, Japan's Minister of Defense announced the deployment of long-range missiles with "enemy base strike capabilities". Some analysts believe this deployment is clearly targeted at China. What's your comment on this?

  • Doorstop - Singapore Prime Minister of Australia 09 Apr 2026
  • Press conference - Lytton, Queensland Prime Minister of Australia 09 Apr 2026

  • PM call with President Trump of the United States: 9 April 2026 Gov.UK 09 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister spoke to President Trump from Qatar this evening.
  • PM meeting with King and Prime Minister of Bahrain: 9 April 2026 Gov.UK 09 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister met with His Majesty the King of Bahrain, Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, and His Royal Highness the Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Bahrain, Salman bin Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa this evening in Manama.
  • PM meeting with President of the UAE: 9 April 2026 Gov.UK 09 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister met the President of the United Arab Emirates, His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in Abu Dhabi this afternoon.
  • Foreign Secretary Mansion House address to City of London 2026 UK FCDO 09 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper delivered the annual Mansion House address to the City of London on 9 April 2026

  • Floods, Displacement and Economic Strain Deepen Humanitarian Needs in Yemen IOM 09 Apr 2026 -- Flooding, displacement and economic hardship continue to drive rising humanitarian needs across Yemen, affecting vulnerable communities nationwide, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) said today, as Othman Belbeisi, IOM Regional Director for the Middle East and North Africa, concluded a field visit to the country.

  • Joint statement MED9 Informal Ministerial Meeting on Security and Resilience in the Mediterranean Spain MFA 09 Apr 2026

  • World News in Brief: Sudan refugee update, child trafficking crisis in South Sudan, 'mystery' illness in Burundi, human rights in Serbia UN News 09 Apr 2026 -- More than a million Sudanese refugees who've fled into neighbouring Chad risk losing out on essential food, shelter and other support due to funding cuts, as the war in their homeland approaches the three-year mark.
